author: juzigu40-ui
title: Config bootstrap non-transactional with destructive read-path side effects

claims.claim: Read path triggers migration when config is missing (load_config -> _load_config_payload -> _migrate_from_state_files)
claims.evidence: config.py:~L144 — _load_config_payload returns _migrate_from_state_files(path) when config.json doesn't exist

claims.claim: Unstabilized migration source order (glob) + first-writer scalar precedence
claims.evidence: config.py:~L396-401 — glob('state-*.json') gives OS-dependent order; _merge_config_value uses first-writer-wins for scalars (key not in config)

claims.claim: Source files are rewritten (del state['config']) before destination durability
claims.evidence: config.py:~L357-381 — _strip_config_from_state_file rewrites each state file inside _migrate_single_state_file, before _migrate_from_state_files calls save_config

claims.claim: Destination write failure is best-effort logged without rollback
claims.evidence: config.py:~L403-409 — save_config in try/except with log_best_effort_failure; state files already stripped at this point

notes: All four claims verified. This is a genuine non-transactional migration with CQS violation. Caveats: one-time migration path, requires config.json absence + write failure to lose data.
